headline: Anger at funeral of Lebanese journalist killed by Israeli army: 'Amal gave voice to the south and the sacrifices endured by this region'
description: Shortly before Donald Trump announced on Thursday a three-week extension of the ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon, friends and relatives of Amal Khalil were burying the reporter, who had been killed by an Israeli strike the previous day.

description: Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu announced a series of measures set to become a bill to be presented by summer.

headline: White House confirms US envoys will head to Pakistan for new rounds of talks with Iran
description: Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will depart for Pakistan on Saturday for a second round of talks with Iran. Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has already arrived in Islamabad ahead of the meeting.

headline: Claude Bessy, star dancer and director of the Paris Opéra's dance school, dies at 93
description: Born into a family of artists in 1932, Claude Bessy, who devoted her life to dance and trained several generations of dancers, died in Paris on Thursday, April 23, at the age of 93.

headline: From 'The Sims' to 'The Last of Us': How video games allow gamers to explore their sexuality
description: Freed from inhibition by the anonymity of the digital world, some gamers find liberation both for their avatars and themselves, leading them to coming out, transitioning or experimenting with their sexuality 'IRL.'

description: The country's most popular politician for the past three years, who started serving as defense minister under Olaf Scholz, is set to unveil a military strategy review on Wednesday aimed at making the Bundeswehr the leading conventional army in Europe. Despite receiving unprecedented financial resources, the institution has been slow to reform.

headline: In Lebanon, Israel establishes an uninhabited buffer zone behind a 'yellow line'
description: The 'demarcation line' drawn by Israel on the third day of the ceasefire has revived memories of the 18-year occupation of Lebanon from 1982 to 2000. The Israeli army has banned Lebanese citizens from entering this 'defense zone,' which covers 6% of the country.

headline: 'Sudan, devastated by two warlords, faces the world's worst humanitarian crisis'
description: Le Monde columnist Alain Frachon reflects on more than three years of conflict in the African country of 50 million people.
